Programs

Survey and Cadastre

MENU

The Survey and Cadastre program is based on the production and measurement of maps at every scale that the country needs, the implementation of cadastral and urban planning maps, and the survey and application of projects such as roads, bridges, dams, tunnels. Graduates, as qualified surveying and cadastral technicians, will have sufficient theoretical and practical knowledge to work in the public or private sector.

The main goal of the program is to train competent "Surveying and Cadastre Technicians" for mapping, measuring, and accounting which are the infrastructure of engineering structures needed by society with rapidly developing technology and increasing population, for projects and applications related to land registry and cadastral information systems, urban activities, and urban transformation applications.

Graduates of the program will be equipped with the knowledge and skills to take on and carry out various tasks in the related units of Municipalities, data management, tunnel and dam construction projects, public enterprises, and private construction companies. Students graduating from this program can be employed in many job fields as technicians and project assistant supervisors.

At our school, all the calculations and measurements that students will encounter while practicing their profession after graduation are carried out practically during the courses, with the equipment park available.

Students graduating from this program have the opportunity to find jobs with their diplomas. In the recruitment process, either in the public or private sector, individuals with a diploma and knowledge in Surveying and Cadastre are especially sought after due to the requirements of the related positions.
